name: OWASP starter policy
note: A STARTING POINT, not an official mapping. These rules approximate control areas from the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (2025) and the OWASP Agentic Security Initiative (Agentic AI: Threats & Mitigations, T1-T15), expressed over Halo Runtime Record v0.1 fields. The most directly relevant threat is T8 Repudiation & Untraceability: a tamper-evident record of what the agent did IS the mitigation. Rename tools/fields to match your agent and your buyer's contract. Run: halo policy <chain.jsonl> owasp-starter.json  (or: halo report <chain.jsonl> --policy owasp-starter.json).

rules.description: Every recorded action carries a timestamp, so it is attributable and non-repudiable. The tamper-evident chain is the control; this rule asserts the evidence exists.
Consequential real-world actions (writes, network egress) must carry an explicit authorization decision, never run unattributed.
Security-category write actions must be human-approved, not auto-allowed by the agent.
TEMPLATE — money movement must be human-approved. Rename action.tool to your real high-impact tools (transfer_funds, issue_refund, ...) or delete if not applicable.
Any action the recorder marked CRITICAL is treated as out of policy until a human reviews it.

rules.source: OWASP LLM01 / Prompt Injection (+ Agentic T6 Intent Breaking)
rules.description: No prompt-injection threat may appear unmitigated in the record.
No credential or sensitive identifier may ever land in a record.
Network actions must not carry SSNs off the boundary.

rules.source: OWASP Agentic T1 / Memory Poisoning (+ LLM04 Data Poisoning) (TEMPLATE)
rules.description: TEMPLATE — expects evidence that a source-integrity / provenance check ran before the agent acted on external content. Rename action.tool to your real integrity-check step, or delete if not applicable.
Every action that touches personal data declares why (purpose limitation).
